title: Parkio Daily for May 25, 2026
teaser: Baby Care Center debuts at Hollywood Studios. Disneyland monitors nearby hazmat situation. Memorial Day crowds build across all parks.

rides.name: Spaceship Earth
rides.note: Memorial Day morning crowds still building

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain
rides.note: Early window before holiday crowds peak

breaking.title: Disneyland operating normally despite nearby hazmat incident
breaking.body: A chemical tank malfunction at a facility in Garden Grove, roughly five miles from Disneyland, has triggered evacuation orders in parts of Orange County. Disney officials confirmed the resort is outside the evacuation zone and remains open with no guest impact. The company is monitoring the situation with local authorities.

parkioInsight.text: If you're feeling uneasy about air quality or the evolving situation, consider delaying your visit or staying flexible with your plans. Local authorities are the source for evacuation zone updates, not park operations.

bignews.title: New Baby Care Center opens at Hollywood Studios
bignews.body: A refreshed Baby Care Center has opened in the newly reimagined Walt Disney Studios courtyard at Hollywood Studios. The space features Disney baby character theming, nursing rooms, changing stations, high chairs, and a kitchenette. Basic infant supplies are sold on-site, subject to availability.

parkioInsight.text: Families with infants now have a quieter, climate-controlled retreat near the Animation Courtyard. The center tends to be less crowded during early afternoon when many guests are at lunch or shows.

topstories.title: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Muppet easter eggs revealed
topstories.body: Disney Parks Blog shared a detailed look at the hidden references and memorabilia inside the reimagined Rock 'n' Roller Coaster queue. Items from MuppetVision 3D, original Electric Mayhem props, and character tributes are layered throughout the G-Force Records building.

parkioInsight.text: Queue detail hunting is easiest during lighter wait windows, typically late evening or right before park close. Standby lines mid-morning often move too quickly to catch everything.

topstories.title: Jessie's Roundup dance party starts early at Magic Kingdom
topstories.body: The Toy Story-themed dance party at Diamond Horseshoe began soft openings ahead of Cool Kids' Summer official kickoff. Jessie hosts the interactive show, which includes Western-themed songs and character interaction.

parkioInsight.text: Soft openings like this often mean lighter crowds and shorter waits for meet-and-greets afterward. Worth stopping by Frontierland mid-afternoon before the dinner rush fills the area.

topstories.title: Victoria & Albert's raises prix fixe menu to $375
topstories.body: Walt Disney World's only Michelin-starred restaurant increased its base prix fixe menu by $80 following recent culinary leadership changes. The new price applies to reservations booked going forward at the Grand Floridian flagship.

parkioInsight.text: At this price point, the value proposition becomes even narrower. If you're considering a splurge, read recent guest reviews carefully to gauge whether the current menu justifies the premium over other Signature Dining options.

icymi.title: EPCOT Imagination Pavilion remains underutilized
icymi.body: Despite Figment's popularity, large portions of the Imagination Pavilion sit empty or underused. The space once housed multiple attractions and interactive areas but now feels sparse outside the Journey Into Imagination ride.

spotlight.title: Memorial Day crowds expected across all parks today
spotlight.body: Monday, May 25 is a federal holiday. Wait times and attendance typically spike by mid-morning at all six Disney parks. Rope drop and late evening hours offer the best windows for lower waits.

parkioInsight.text: Holiday Mondays usually hit peak congestion between 11am and 4pm. Arrive before official opening or plan a midday break to dodge the worst of it. Evening hours often thin out after dinner, especially at EPCOT and Hollywood Studios.
